title: Narrow platform merge support scope

sources.reason: Contains the current platform support claims and the GitLab Free compatibility design that must be narrowed.
Records the prior review findings and residual risks for the approved design.
Defines canonical Repository Governance Layer and Recommendation terminology.
Defines the one Work Item, one branch, one PR review unit and template-repository branch base.
The user explicitly approved deleting the detailed GitLab Free compatibility design and retaining only a clear non-correspondence statement.

riskAssessment.reason: This is a documentation-only narrowing of supported platform claims; the main risk is leaving contradictory GitLab Free compatibility text elsewhere in the same design.

executionDecision.reason: The user approved the exact documentation scope and confirmed that GitLab Free should be stated as not corresponding rather than described in detail.

preReviewWarnings: Confirm every synthetic compatibility, protected coordinator, credential, and GitLab Free safety claim is removed from the design.
Confirm GitLab Free remains explicitly visible as unsupported for target-integrated parallel merge safety rather than disappearing silently.
Confirm GitHub Merge Queue and GitLab Premium or Ultimate Merge Train behavior remains unchanged.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The design goal, decisions, capability matrix, profiles, testing strategy, and rollout sequence no longer claim a GitLab Free synthetic compatibility gate.
The design explicitly states that GitLab Free does not correspond to the target-integrated parallel merge safety modes defined by this document and is limited to head-only diagnostic validation.
The detailed GitLab Free coordinator, credential, protected push, retry, reconciliation, and synthetic evidence sections are removed rather than retained as an implementation protocol.
GitHub Merge Queue and GitLab Premium or Ultimate Merge Train remain the native platform paths described by the design, while AI Cockpit Core remains platform-neutral.
No runtime scripts, tests, templates, CI files, Makefile targets, or unrelated documentation are changed.
guidelines: Do not imply that GitLab Free provides or will receive an equivalent parallel merge safety guarantee in this design.
Keep the unsupported-platform statement concise and capability-based rather than documenting a replacement implementation.
Preserve the distinction between target-integrated candidate verification and head-only diagnostic checks.
Keep AI Cockpit Core platform-neutral and avoid claiming runtime behavior is already implemented.

intent.problem: The approved design describes a detailed GitLab Free synthetic merge coordinator whose eligibility and trust guarantees are difficult to bind atomically. The user has decided that GitLab Free should not be treated as a corresponding parallel merge safety platform in this design.
constraints: Remove the detailed GitLab Free compatibility protocol instead of attempting to repair or expand it.
State the non-correspondence explicitly so readers do not infer support from omission.
Preserve the existing GitHub Merge Queue, GitLab Premium or Ultimate Merge Train, and platform-neutral Core boundaries.
Limit this Work Item to documentation and governance evidence; do not change runtime or CI behavior.
Use origin as the template-repository base remote and main as the base branch; baseCommit records the fetched origin/main commit.
intent.rationale: A narrow, explicit support boundary is safer and easier to maintain than a speculative GitLab Free automation design that cannot provide the same platform-native candidate and freshness guarantees.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: The support-boundary decision is explicit and approved before editing the design.
Runtime, CI, installer, credential, and test implementation remain outside this documentation Work Item.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
